note: BRAIN layer — follow-up copy queue. HANDS layer sends these via SMTP. Do NOT send to leads in reply_classifications.json suppression list.

followups.original_subject: Tom — your Ripon carpentry site isn't mobile-friendly
followups.original_pain_point: Website not mobile-friendly — losing mobile visitors
followups.subject: Quick question about Ripon Road Carpentry
followups.body:
Tom,

Follow-up on my note from last week about your site.

I had a look at your Google listing — you've got great work in the photos but no way for someone to request a quote online. Most jobs your size start with 'get a quote', and right now there's no button for that.

Happy to add a simple quote form to the existing site — takes a day, could mean 3-5 extra enquiries a month.

Worth a quick chat?

[Your name]
followups.notes: Day 7 — shift from mobile issue to a new angle (missing quote CTA). Keeps it fresh, not a nag.

followups.original_subject: Nidderdale Pet Supplies — site not showing in mobile searches
followups.subject: One thing I noticed about Nidderdale Pet Supplies
followups.body:
Hi,

Dropping a quick line — noticed that Nidderdale Pet Supplies doesn't appear when you search 'pet shop near me' on a phone in the Pateley Bridge area.

Your Google Maps listing is active but the site isn't passing the mobile test, which pushes you down in local results.

I do a free local SEO snapshot for independent shops like yours — takes 10 minutes and I'll tell you exactly what's holding the ranking back.

Interested?

[Your name]
followups.notes: No owner name — keep generic. New angle: local search visibility rather than mobile UX.

followups.original_subject: David — your Wetherby accountancy site has an SSL issue
followups.subject: Harewood Accountants — one question
followups.body:
David,

Just a quick one — do you get much business from Google?

I ask because Harewood Accountants isn't in the top 5 results for 'accountant Wetherby', and the SSL issue I mentioned last week is one reason why. Google actively ranks secure sites higher.

For an accountancy firm, trust matters twice — once online, once in person. The SSL fix helps with both.

Happy to do a 10-minute screen share and show you exactly where you're sitting in local rankings.

[Your name]
followups.notes: Professional audience — shift to business impact angle (Google rankings) rather than just the technical SSL issue.

followups.original_subject: Spen Valley Tyres — website flagged as Not Secure
followups.subject: Re: Spen Valley Tyres — website flagged as Not Secure
followups.body:
Hi,

Just bumping this up in case it got buried.

The SSL fix for spenvalleytyres.co.uk is a small job — happy to look at it this week if it's useful.

[Your name]
followups.notes: Day 3 bump — short, no pressure. Just a thread reply to keep it in context.

followups.original_subject: Priya — your alterations site doesn't show on mobile
followups.subject: Re: your alterations site doesn't show on mobile
followups.body:
Priya,

Just following up in case my last note got lost.

I checked again — the site is still failing mobile. Happy to show you what a quick fix looks like if it'd be helpful.

[Your name]
followups.notes: Day 3. Owner name known. Keep it personal and brief.

followups.original_subject: Kirkstall Joinery — no website hurts your Google visibility
followups.subject: Re: Kirkstall Joinery — no website hurts your Google visibility
followups.body:
Hi,

Quick follow-up — just checking this landed okay.

If you'd like to see what a one-page trades site looks like for a joinery business, happy to send over an example.

[Your name]
followups.notes: Day 3. No owner name. Offer a tangible asset (example site) to prompt reply.

followups.original_subject: Graham — Ilkley Ironmongery site is 'Not Secure'
followups.subject: Closing the loop — Ilkley Ironmongery
followups.body:
Graham,

I've reached out a couple of times about the SSL issue on the ironmongery site — won't keep chasing if it's not useful.

If the timing's ever right down the line, you know where I am.

All the best,
[Your name]
followups.notes: NOT DUE TODAY — flagged for 11 April. Pre-written breakup email for Day 14. Queued in advance.
